title: Motor coach bus catches on fire on I-95 in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A motor coach bus caught fire on I-95 in Hampton, NH, Sunday; all occupants evacuated safely.
Captain Damien Savin reported no injuries, and the fire is believed to have started in the wheel area.
Hampton Fire advises travelers to expect delays and consider alternate routes while responders work.

title: Cambridge man who threw cat out of window sentenced for animal cruelty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jaydan Depina, 22, pleaded guilty to six counts of animal cruelty for abusing a cat in Cambridge in 2025.
Depina was sentenced to 90 days in prison followed by two years of probation and required mental health treatment.
The cat, Sage, suffered multiple injuries but has since recovered and been adopted into a new home.

title: Man killed on Boston highway after head-on crash with wrong-way driver
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Haverhill man in his 20s killed in head-on crash on I-93 with wrong-way driver.
81-year-old Antone Carvalho drove wrong way, causing multiple crashes before hitting a Chevrolet Cruze.
Carvalho will receive a court summons; this is the third fatal wrong-way incident in a week.

title: Jarren Duran had another confrontation with a fan at Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jarren Duran confronted a heckling fan during the Red Sox's 4-1 win over the Yankees at Fenway Park.
Security removed a heckling Yankees fan in the ninth inning after Duran's confrontation on Saturday.
Duran has faced multiple fan-related incidents, including a gesture to a fan and previous altercations over his mental health.

title: Brookline party store, Chinese restaurant both close after decades in business
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Golden Temple, a Chinese restaurant in Brookline, closed after 66 years, known for its late-night dining and E Room bar.
Party Favors, a bakery and party store also in Brookline, will close mid-July after 35 years, with discounts offered until then.
Both businesses announced closures through social media, thanking customers for their support, but didn't specify reasons for closing.

title: Celtics reportedly sign Ron Harper Jr. to 3-year deal
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics re-sign Ron Harper Jr. to a three-year, $9 million contract, declining a $2.6 million team option.
Ron Harper Jr., 26, appeared in 26 games last season, averaging 4.0 points and 1.7 rebounds for the Celtics.
Harper had a breakout season, highlighted by a 27-point game against Orlando and a start in Game 7 against Philadelphia.

title: 2 JP residents bitten by raccoons in separate incidents, animal control says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two residents were bitten by raccoons in separate incidents over the weekend in Jamaica Plain.
The bites occurred at Jamaica Pond and near Kenton Street; both victims were hospitalized.
Animal control officers have not found the raccoons and continue to patrol the affected areas.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the Norway-France World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick, leading France to a 4-1 victory over Norway at Boston Stadium.
The match drew over 64,000 spectators, showcasing lively fan scenes and standout soccer moments in Foxborough.
Kylian Mbappe contributed two assists, while Norwegian fans and players like Andreas Schjelderup were highlighted.

title: 5 prospects to watch during Bruins Development Camp
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Dean Letourneau is a standout prospect with a high ceiling, participating in the Bruins Development Camp at Warrior Ice Arena.
Cooper Simpson showcased scoring prowess in the USHL and will join the University of North Dakota this fall.
Max Lundgren, a promising goaltender, will be under watch after an MVP performance in Merrimack's Hockey East title win.

title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eleven people were injured, including one seriously, when a duck boat overturned in Cambridge on Saturday at 10 Museum Way.
The duck boat overturned after a tow rope snapped while being pulled from the Charles River by another vessel.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations for the day and is collaborating with authorities to investigate the incident.
